name: Black Bean Chili with Oranges
recipeIngredient: 1 pound dried black beans, sorted for debris and rinsed
3/4 teaspoon crushed dried hot red chilies
6 cardamom pods, hulls removed (1/4 teaspoon seed)
2 1/2 pounds oranges for zest, juice, and slices
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine onions, garlic, and oil in a lidded 6-quart saucepan over high heat.
2. Stir often until onions are tinged with brown, about 8 minutes.
3. Add remaining ingredients (excluding zest, juice, and slices) to the onions and mix well.
4. Bring to a boil over high heat.
5. Reduce heat to simmer and cover. Simmer until beans are tender to bite, about 1 1/2 to 2 hours.
6. While beans simmer, remove 2 teaspoons of zest, squeeze 1/2 cup of juice from the oranges, peel and remove the white membrane from the rest of the fruit, and slice thinly crosswise (removing seeds).
7. After beans become tender, raise heat to high, uncover, and boil until most of the liquid evaporates, about 10 - 15 minutes.
8. Reduce heat and stir occasionally as the mixture thickens. Remove beans from heat and stir in 1 teaspoon of zest and the juice.
9. Ladle beans into serving bowls.
10. Place sliced fruit equally on top of the beans in each bowl.
11. Add to taste: sour cream, fresh cilantro sprigs, salt. Garnish with the remaining 1/4 teaspoon of zest.
